pattern: Fix the third position of each ordered quadruplet and pre-tally how often every value appears after it, so the fourth position's required value can be looked up instantly instead of scanned for.
hints: The four indices must stay in strictly increasing order, but the values themselves do not need to be sorted - only their positions matter.
For a fixed third index, build a frequency table of every value that appears strictly after it; that table answers, for any target sum, how many valid fourth indices exist.
With the third index and its after-table fixed, loop over every possible first and second index before it, look up first+second+third in the after-table, and add that count to the running total.

edge_inputs.description: no matching quadruplet despite close values
repeated small values produce several matches
minimum length array with no match
minimum length array with exactly one match
repeated triple summing to the last element
identical values that never satisfy the sum condition

stress.description: random arrays near the max length and value range
